This mortal does not put on immortality] That is, this physical body, subject to death, does not become immortal- no longer subject to death- until after Christ rises from the grave. (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Robert L. Millet, Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon, 4 vols., 3:, p.295)

This mortal does not put on immortality] That is, this physical body, subject to death, does not become immortal- no longer subject to death- until after Christ rises from the grave. (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Robert L. Millet, Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon, 4 vols., 3:, p.295)

This corruption does not put on incorruption] That is, this physical body subject to disease and decay, does not become incorrupt- no longer subject to those conditions- until after Christ rises from the grave. (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Robert L. Millet, Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon, 4 vols., 3:, p.295)
